Title: Digital voter registration and hybrid election system
KeyTerms: Elections, Civic Participation, Voter Registration, Biometric Verification, Data Exchange, Digital Inclusion, Accessibility, Offline Authentication
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multi-factor authentication for election officials and digital systems
Description:
Papua New Guinea's current voter registration and election processes suffer from fragmented identity data, unreliable or outdated voter rolls with duplicate and missing records, and severe access challenges for remote communities. These gaps produce operational and democratic problems including inaccurate electoral rolls that undermine confidence in results, manual paper-based verification and counting processes that are slow, error-prone, and easily disputed, and exclusion of remote populations with poor connectivity. Weak cross-sector identity interoperability between civil registries, telecommunications, and financial institutions prevents fast eKYC and SIM validation needed for trustworthy voter roll preparation. 
This use case proposes a hybrid election system integrating SevisPass Digital ID, SevisDEx (Data Exchange), and National ID (NID) with census data and eKYC processes to create verified voter identity across the entire voting population. The system combines online authentication via SevisDEx with offline Android applications for voter registration in rural areas lacking internet access. 
Traditional ballot counting will eventually transition to digital platforms. This approach reduces duplicate and inflated rolls through authoritative identity linking with biometrics and probabilistic matching, creating single canonical voter records. It improves accessibility by allowing offline-capable wallets and enumerator Android apps to enroll remote citizens without continuous internet. 
It speeds processes and reduces costs through consented, auditable attribute exchange (age, residency) automating eligibility checks and shortening queues. The hybrid model is uniquely suitable for PNG's current economic and social structure, revolutionizing voting systems and eliminating recurring election-period issues across all government agencies.

Title: SADC Digital Farmer ID: Regional Biometric Identity and Resource Access System for Farmers
KeyTerms: Agriculture, Digital Inclusion & Accessibility, Regional Integration, Blockchain, IoT Sensors, Subsidy Management, Food Security
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multi-factor authentication at Smart Village Hubs and digital platform access for resource applications
Description:
Southern African farmers lack centralized identity systems and accurate land records, preventing subsidy, loan, and insurance access. Fraudulent claims divert resources; farmers lack climate/price resilience or productivity tools. 
SADC Digital Farmer ID provides revolutionary biometric system empowering farmers through unique digital IDs via fingerprints/facial recognition linked to Farmer Registry. Centralized database contains land size, crops, livestock, water usage, yield history. Features include streamlined subsidy/loan/insurance access, market connections with buyers/cooperatives, blockchain ensuring tamper-proof land/resource records, AI providing personalized advice on planting/pest control/water management, IoT sensors monitoring soil/water usage. 
Smart Village Hubs with biometric scanners provide accessible registration, service access, and training. By 2030, transforms 100 million farmers' lives, enhances food security, ensures sustainable growth, and empowers remote farmers through precision agriculture and market access.

Title: Digital ID-based tax compliance system
KeyTerms: Public Finance, Governance, Tax Administration, Fraud Prevention, Financial Data Integration, Blockchain, AI-Driven Audits
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 3 - Multi-factor biometric authentication for tax authority access and financial data verification
Description:
Senegal loses 526.4 billion FCFA annually (3.4% GDP) to tax evasion with 97% of businesses informal and only 6.5% registered under NINEA. Weak enforcement and taxpayer verification enable fraud, limiting infrastructure/social investments. 
QUANTIC integrates digital ID infrastructure with NINEA taxpayer system and financial databases using biometric verification, blockchain for tamper-proof records, and AI-driven fraud detection. Mandatory biometric-verified digital ID for business registration prevents duplicate tax identities and fraudulent shell companies. 
Automated taxpayer verification with real-time business owner verification enables AI-driven audits detecting declaration inconsistencies. Cross-agency data sharing ensures secure interoperability between tax authorities, banks, customs, and business registries. 
Digital payment and electronic invoicing integration ensures transaction traceability. System eliminates fake identities, reduces fraud, enhances government revenue, lowers administrative costs, and brings informal businesses into formal economy promoting financial inclusion and economic growth.

Title: Digital ID-based property ownership and transfer system
KeyTerms: Public Services, Property Management, Blockchain, Smart Contracts, Fraud Prevention, Asset Security, Escrow Management
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 3 - Hardware and cryptographic-based authentication for property transfer transactions using blockchain
Description:
Eastern Africa's property ownership verification and transfer processes are cumbersome and fraud-susceptible, making transactions insecure and inefficient while generating disputes and eroding trust. 
SafeZone Solutions leverages digital ID to streamline and secure property management. Digital IDs link to property records ensuring secure, fraud-proof ownership verification. Property sales enable secure ownership transfer from seller's digital ID to the buyer's through a blockchain-enabled platform. AI models detect platform fraud. 
Platform provides space for users listing and selling properties with buyer verification of legitimacy and ownership before initiating transactions. Every transaction records in tamper-proof blockchain ledger providing complete property history for audits and legal purposes. Escrow manages transaction funds while smart contracts issue ownership certificates. 
MOSIP platform prototype development gains technical insights, while government database integration ensures data accuracy. Blockchain technology ensures scalability and reliability. Secure digital platform reduces fraud, increases efficiency, and enhances transparency, making property management accessible and trustworthy.

Title: Digital ID-based platform for verified employment records
KeyTerms: Education, Employment, Public Administration, Fraud Prevention, Credential Verification, AI-Powered Matching, Real-Time Data Management
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multi-factor authentication for job seeker and employer access to verified credential systems
Description:
West African labor markets face diploma fraud and weak credential checks. Studies show that 68% of CVs mislead employers, and 29% list incorrect qualifications. This raises hiring costs and slows hiring procedure. 
The proposed use case provides a digital ID-based platform for employment records. Individuals seeking employment create their profile on the platform. 
Original certificates are uploaded by the individuals. The platform verifies qualifications via diploma-issuing authorities and skill agencies and creates trusted digital profiles. 
Employers post jobs on a single platform and receive verified applications. Government use the data to plan employment policies and target social support.

Title: Digital platform for youth entrepreneurship support
KeyTerms: Entrepreneurship, Financial Services, Financial Inclusion, Digital Inclusion & Accessibility, Mentorship, Blockchain, AI-Driven Recommendations
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multi-factor authentication for loan applications and financial transactions
Description:
Young entrepreneurs in Eastern Africa face barriers to finance, mentorship, and business networks. Absence of formal identification prevents them from opening bank accounts and securing loans. 
This use case proposes a digital platform that uses digital ID to facilitate access to finance, mentorship, and business networks. Entrepreneurs create their profile on the platform. These profiles show their skills, business ideas, and financial history. This helps them access microloans, crowdfunding, and grants. 
The platform also offers online courses, mentorship, and networking support. It builds skills and supports business growth. It reaches underserved youth and expands their access to economic opportunities.

Title: Digital access to the administrative documents using MOSIP platform
KeyTerms: Public Services, Administrative Services, Digital Inclusion & Accessibility, OCR Technology, AI Chatbot, Biometric Authentication, Data Validation
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multi-factor authentication for government document requests and official submissions
Description:
Senegal lacks formal identity documents for 40% of its citizens. This limits access to education, healthcare, employment, and social programs. Rural citizens face long travel, high costs, delays and risks of document loss or fraud. 
This proposed use case creates a centralized platform as a simple way to access administrative documents. Citizens create their accounts on the platform using MOSIP authentication. They access the platform via their MOSIP Single Sign-On (SSO) and select the documents they want. The platform uses OCR to extract and validate data. It sends requests to relevant authorities for approval. 
Once approved, the platform issues secure PDFs with certification for download. It also provides status notifications at each stage. An AI chatbot answers questions and guides users.

Title: SunuAssistance: Revolutionizing Emergency Response with Digital ID and Real-Time Geolocation
KeyTerms: AI-enabled emergency response system
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multi-factor authentication for emergency alert triggering and emergency services dashboard access
Description:
Senegal faces delays in emergency response. Communication gaps, poor location tracking, and weak case prioritization cause these delays. Studies show that effective emergency management could prevent up to 36% of disabilities and 45% of deaths.
The proposed use case idea suggests a platform to improve response efficiency through digital ID and AI. Users authenticate through MOSIP digital ID and select the required service, such as police, medical, or fire support. The system shares the user’s location automatically with responders.
AI-based bots guide users with first-aid steps while they wait. Users can track response status in real time.
The platform also supports direct communication through chat. This platform reduces reliance on verbal descriptions, improves resource allocation, and shortens response time

Title: Digital medical identity for efficient healthcare delivery
KeyTerms: Healthcare, Digital Health Records, Patient Identity, Interoperability, Mobile Access, QR Code Verification, Data Synchronization
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multi-factor authentication for healthcare provider access to patient medical files
Description:
West and Central Africa lack digital medical identities. Digitalization in healthcare stands at only 17.75%. This causes treatment delays and proliferation/loss of physical medical documents. 
The proposed use case idea creates a mobile application that creates a unique digital medical identity for each patient. It stores medical records in a centralized place. Patients can securely access their medical history online. 
Each patient's medical identity is linked to their unique digital ID. The linked medical records are accessible via a secure QR code. 
The system syncs electronic medical records regularly across healthcare facilities. It also enables data exchange between healthcare providers. Patients control access to their medical information.

Title: Targeted and transparent agricultural subsidy delivery
KeyTerms: Agriculture, Fraud Prevention, Data-Driven Decision Making, Machine Learning, Digital Identity, Subsidy Management, Food Security
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multi-factor authentication for subsidy program access and benefit verification
Description:
Sub-Saharan African governments face agricultural subsidy related frauds. There are cases of elite capture and non-targeted allocation, which limit food security and poverty reduction. 
The proposed use case idea uses a digital ID-based single sign-on to verify the farmer's identity. It then uses machine learning for data-driven subsidy allocation. The platform removes fraudulent beneficiaries through secure digital ID checks. The system analyzes farmer data to identify vulnerable groups. It prioritizes those who need support the most.

Title: Personalized skincare and monitoring solutions
KeyTerms: Healthcare, Biometric Authentication, AI/Machine Learning, Wearable Sensors, Personalized Medicine, Privacy & Data Protection
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 1 - Single-factor authentication for accessing personalized skincare recommendations and profiles
Description:
North Africa lacks personalized skincare solutions. Many people use unsuitable products and spend resources without results. Strong sun exposure leads to early aging and sunburns. This  affects skin health and self-confidence. 
This idea proposes use of wearable sensors and digital ID to address this gap.  Sensors measure changes in skin layers i.e. epidermis, dermis and hypodermis. These detect hydration, oil levels, and pigmentation. The system identifies skin types and provides instant feedback. Digital ID stores each user’s skin profile. Users can share these profiles with dermatologists. They provide accurate advise based on verified data. Pharmacies can also suggest suitable products.

Title: Secure digital voting with biometric authentication
KeyTerms: Public Services, Civic Participation, Biometric Authentication, Electoral Security, Blockchain, Accessibility, Fraud Prevention
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 3 - Hardware-based biometric fingerprint authentication combined with digital card verification at polling stations
Description:
West African elections face serious challenges. Fraud such as multiple voting and ballot stuffing remains common., Rural and disabled voters often face exclusion. 
The proposed use case idea integrates national identity cards (CNI) and fingerprint biometrics for voter verification at digital polling stations. Each voter inserts the card for initial verification and then scan fingerprints for confirmation. Once verified, the voter selects a candidate through a simple interface. The system encrypts each vote and stores it on a blockchain. 
The system also proposes that the election authority can adopt mobile solar-powered polling stations to serve rural areas. These station can operate offline and can sync again when internet is available.

Title: Secure digital prescription and patient identity system
KeyTerms: Healthcare, Cybersecurity, Privacy & Data Protection, Biometric Authentication, Blockchain, Prescription Security
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multi-factor authentication for healthcare providers accessing prescription systems
Description:
Malawi's healthcare system lacks secure identity verification and fragmented health records. Due to which prescription fraud and medication errors remain common. 
This use case idea proposes blockchain-powered prescriptions records. It gives real-time access to authorized users. The system links respective digital ID with patient and healthcare provider profile. 
Healthcare providers link each prescriptions to a verified patient profile through digital ID. Pharmacies can verify at the point of service. The system ensures prescriptions cannot be altered without detection. Privacy is preserved through permissioned access controls. The system is designed to scale across Malawi's healthcare network as adoption grows.

Title: Automated student loan repayment through digital identity and tax integration
KeyTerms: Education, Financial Services, Social Protection, Data Exchange, Employment Verification, Automated Workflows
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 1 - System-level authentication for government agency data exchange via tax records
Description:
Botswana's student loan system is based on manual checks. Repayment of the loan is also tracked manually. This leads to missed repayments and limits funds for new scholarships. There is no reliable way to determine the employment status or to request repayment. 
The proposed use case links Botswana's Unified Services Digital ID (BoSDID) with Tax Identification Numbers (TINs). It uses data from BURS (Botswana Unified Revenue Service) to track employment status. The system detects when a graduate starts work and triggers loan repayment automatically. 
It uses real-time tax data and removes the need for self-reporting. This solution reduces administrative effort. The system no longer depends on outdated contact details. It creates a reliable and sustainable student loan cycle and supports continuous scholarship funding.

Title: Fast and secure airport check-in
KeyTerms: Transportation & Logistics, Border Security, Biometric Authentication, Facial Recognition, Accessibility, Speed Optimization
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 3 - Hardware-based facial biometric authentication achieving 99.55% accuracy with cryptographic verification
Description:
Airports in Morocco face delays of 15-30 minutes at passport checking points. Manual photo checks create errors. This leads to confusion for both travellers and staff. 
This proposed use case idea uses facial authentication linked to digital ID. The system replaces manual checks and improves accuracy to 99.55%. It reduces errors and speeds up passenger flow.

Title: PNG Data Gateway App: Decentralized Cryptographic Identity Integration for Service Sector Inclusion
KeyTerms: Digital Inclusion & Accessibility, Blockchain, Decentralized Identity, Data Sovereignty, Cryptographic Security, Self-Sovereign Identity
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Cryptographic-based authentication using blockchain with offline mobile wallet support
Description:
PNG launched its National ID project in 2014 with funding of K500 million. By September 2025, it had registered only 3.8 million people and printed 1.39 million cards. This leaves most citizens without access to essential services. 
This use case idea proposes a new digital ID approach. It uses  blockchain-based identity with Decentralized Identifiers (DIDs) and Verifiable Credentials (VCs).
  Citizens control their data. They can authenticate, verify, and share identity details without a central database. 
Blockchain ensures tamper-proof, auditable, and privacy-preserving records. The standardized application enables government departments, health workers, and businesses to capture biometric data (face/fingerprint) linked to new digital IDs. 
SevisPass functionality supports authentication, instant credential verification, and consent-based data sharing. A community-driven incentive model distributes solar lights, mosquito nets, and free SIM cards to encourage voluntary, inclusive adoption.

Title: Smart Police Clearance Portal with SevisPass Verification and Facial Authentication
KeyTerms: Public Services, Law Enforcement, Biometric Authentication, Facial Recognition, Digital Verification, Cybersecurity, Accessibility
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multi-factor authentication combining SevisPass login and facial biometric verification
Description:
PNG citizens face delays when they apply for police clearance. The process requires NID numbers, facial photos, and in-person fingerprint collection. Applicants must visit the National Crime Records Office, which increases cost and travel time. In some provinces, the process takes up to three weeks.
The proposed smart police clearance portal simplifies this process through SevisPass. Citizens log in with SevisPass single sign-on, and the system fills registration forms with verified identity data.
Users make payments online and submit facial scans through SevisPass. The system matches these scans with government-verified identity records. This removes the need for on-site fingerprinting. Citizens complete the process through a phone or computer camera

Title: SevisPass-Enabled Digital Access System for Secure Public and Private Service Delivery
KeyTerms: Public Services, Service Delivery, Digital Access Portal, Biometric Authentication, Rural Accessibility, Data Sharing, Interoperability
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multi-factor biometric and digital credential authentication for service portal access
Description:
Many citizens in PNG cannot access essential services because identity checks are unreliable. Records remain fragmented, and manual processes create long queues and repeated registrations. Rural citizens travel long distances and carry physical documents that are easy to lose or damage.
This solution offers a unified digital platform built on SevisPass. It supports service delivery across government agencies and businesses. Citizens authenticate through biometrics-based ID checks and consent-based data sharing on a web and mobile interface.
Users can access permits, personal records, appointments, social support, and work or financial verification with a verified SevisPass ID. The system reduces fraud and duplicate identities. It removes the need for physical documents and improves privacy.

Title: Financial access through digital identity
KeyTerms: Financial Services, Digital Payments, Financial Inclusion, Biometric Authentication, NFC Cards, POS Terminal, eKYC, AML Compliance
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multi-factor authentication combining biometrics and digital credentials for wallet access and transactions
Description:
About 75% of adults in PNG do not have bank access because they lack a reliable identity. Birth certificate coverage remains as low as 13%, which worsens exclusion.
This use case idea proposes a platform that integrates SevisPass to verify identity and meet KYC requirements during the bank account opening. SevisPass provides verified and portable identities, so users can confirm their identity without physical documents.
Users log in to the platform through multi-factor authentication to securely access their bank accounts. The system verifies national IDs, biometrics, and supporting documents. After authentication, users can access payments, merchant services, savings, and tokenized assets. Businesses reduce onboarding costs, while rural communities gain access to essential financial services.

Title: SevisPass Connect: Building a Digital Device for Remote Citizens with Offline-First Authentication
KeyTerms: Digital Inclusion & Accessibility, Offline Authentication, Rural Access, Cryptographic Signatures, Mobile Device, Service Delivery, Identity Verification
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 1 - Device-level single-factor authentication with cryptographic signatures
Description:
Many citizens in PNG cannot access formal services because they lack a verifiable identity, particularly in rural areas where service delivery is weak, and KYC processes are slow. 
This use case addresses these challenges through an offline-first mobile authentication system. Users create accounts online. The system then sends secure authentication tokens, encrypted data, and encryption keys to their devices. Users can store, edit, view, and share identity information without internet access. 
They can complete service forms, such as SIM registration or government assistance, offline and submit once connectivity is restored. They can also capture physical IDs, such as NID cards and birth certificates, offline and sync them when connected. The system relies on device-level security and cryptographic signatures.

Title: A verified digital marketplace for farmers
KeyTerms: Agriculture, Digital Marketplace, Farmer Verification, E-Commerce, Mobile-First, SMS/USSD, Financial Inclusion, Digital Inclusion
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 1 - Single-factor authentication acceptable for farmer platform access and basic transactions via USSD
Description:
PNG farmers lack verified identities and rely on feature phones. Internet access remains limited, preventing farmers from using digital platforms to stay updated on market trends. Buyers also struggle to find trusted suppliers. 
This proposed platform connects farmers and buyers through a secure system. Farmers register with SevisPass, which verifies identity and removes false listings. Community agents collect farmer data offline and sync it once connectivity is restored. Buyers use the platform’s website or app to view structured produce data, which they can filter by location, crop, quantity, availability, and price. 
Buyers contact farmers through SMS or phone call. Payments go directly to the farmers’ bank accounts via SevisWallet.

Title: Digital identity for secure financial services
KeyTerms: Financial Services, Digital Identity, Fraud Prevention, Loan Processing, Financial Inclusion, Biometric Authentication, Payments
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multi-factor authentication for loan applications and financial transactions
Description:
PNG faces high fraud levels and identity theft. This results in low financial inclusion as institutions follow manual verification to provide financial services. Citizens and businesses face scams and delays as a result.

This use case proposes a secure digital ecosystem to address these challenges. The ecosystem includes mobile apps, institutional portals, and API integration. Users create digital profiles using SevisPass, a digital ID and biometric verification system. Banks send requests through the bank portal, and users approve data sharing via mobile applications. Verified data enables instant loan processing.

Title: Centralized medical database for patient records
KeyTerms: Healthcare, Digital Health Records, Patient Identity, Data Interoperability, Medical Continuity, Cloud Infrastructure, Real-Time Access
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multi-factor biometric and authentication for healthcare provider access to patient records
Description:
PNG's healthcare system relies on paper-based patient records. There are delays, repeated tests, and diagnostic errors due to loss and damage of these records.  
The proposed use case idea provides a secure, cloud-based central repository. It stores patient medical histories, lab results, prescriptions, immunizations, and hospital visit records. Healthcare providers and patients access the system via SevisPass, which uses multi-factor authentication. When a patient visits a facility, staff retrieve health data in real time after authentication. 
Patients also view their records, test results, and appointments through the user portal. SevisDEx enables data exchange across facilities and ensures interoperability across PNG’s healthcare system.

Title: National telemedicine system powered by SevisPass digital ID
KeyTerms: Healthcare, Telemedicine, Digital Consultations, Rural Access, Price Transparency, E-Prescriptions, Mobile Device, Digital Inclusion
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multi-factor authentication for healthcare provider access
Description:
Papua New Guinea faces major gaps in healthcare access. Rural communities lack doctors and facilities. Around 87% of rural communities lack regular access to a doctor, and there is one doctor per 10,000 people in these areas. They have to travel long distances for basic care. Patients pay high fees and cannot verify the doctor's qualifications.
This use case proposes a national telemedicine platform. Patients consult SevisPass-verified doctors through secure digital channels. Doctors are onboarded on the platform by the national healthcare authority based on their qualifications. Patients can book online appointments for a reasonable fee. Doctors issue digitally signed prescriptions that patients can store digitally and share at the pharmacy through QR codes. SevisWallet handles payments. The system sends medication reminders linked to patient IDs.
SevisDEx enables secure, consent-based sharing of medical records. The platform also shows nearby verified clinics and supports emergency ambulance requests with location and identity details.

Title: Ensuring transparency in agricultural project acquittals through farmer verification
KeyTerms: Agriculture, Rural Development, Project Accountability, Farmer Verification, Financial Inclusion, Data Sharing, Transparency
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 1 - Single-factor authentication acceptable for farmer project participation checkpoints
Description:
Farmers in Papua New Guinea have limited extension support. Existing agricultural projects often face fraud from fake “paper farmers”. These fake “paper farmers” submit false proposals and access grants. Officials find it hard to verify projects in remote areas. This leads to fund misuse.

This use case proposes SevisPass to verify farmers and projects. Farmers give consent and confirm their identity before they submit proposals. After approval, each farmer verifies participation through periodic check-ins. The system records each step through secure identity checks. Final verification confirms project completion and real farmer involvement.

This approach blocks fake applications and flags suspicious projects. It improves transparency and accountability in public spending. Communities can track projects and ask local custodians questions. Funds reach real farmers, not just reports.

Title: National digital identity verification gateway for social media
KeyTerms: Public Services, Cybersecurity, Digital Safety, Anti-Fraud, Account Verification, Social Media Security, Privacy & Data Protection, Digital Inclusion
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multi-factor authentication through SevisPortal for all social media account creation and access
Description:
Papua New Guinea faces a digital safety crisis. Fake profiles, identity theft, and cyberbullying spread because platforms do not verify users. Women and public figures face harassment from anonymous accounts that no one can trace.
This use case proposes SevisPass as the national identity layer for all social media platforms. Users must verify their identity via OTP-based verification of the Sevis Pass digital ID when they activate an account. Users must verify their existing accounts or else face deactivation. The system uses SevisPass for authentication and SevisDEx for secure verification and real-time checks.
This approach removes fake and anonymous accounts. It creates a safer online environment. The Department of Information and Communications Technology can monitor activity without exposing personal data.

Title: Education to workforce identity lifecycle powered by SevisPass
KeyTerms: Education, Digital Credentials, Teacher Verification, Workforce Development, Certificate Authenticity, Interoperability, Data Sharing, Fraud Prevention
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multi-factor authentication for school administrators and educators
Description:
Papua New Guinea's education sector faces cases of forged certificates, identity fraud, and unreliable academic records. This prevents students from applying for scholarships in time and prevents their entry into higher education. Paper-based systems cause lost transcripts, slow verification, and inconsistent national data. Teacher qualifications are also difficult to authenticate. 
This use case idea proposes to create a platform to provide a lifelong digital education identity linking every student and teacher. Each student and teacher will be verified by their SevisPass IDs throughout their education journey. Students receive digital credentials instantly shareable with schools, scholarship boards, and employers. All educational verifications occur exclusively through SevisPass, replacing paper-based methods. Teacher identities and qualifications are verified, preventing unqualified educators from entering classrooms. After graduation, verified education records automatically link to a national employment hub, creating a transparent pathway from learning to employment. This integrated approach prevents fraud, ensures data accuracy, improves workforce readiness, and establishes PNG's first fully verified education-to-employment ecosystem.

Title: Supporting rural health workers in maternal care
KeyTerms: Maternal Health, Rural Health Systems, Health Worker Support, Offline Resources, Digital Inclusion, Community Health, Training & Development
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 1 - Single-factor authentication acceptable for healthcare workers accessing offline maternal care resources
Description:
Papua New Guinea's geography and dispersed population make maternal healthcare difficult in rural areas. Health workers often lack training, real-time support, and access to updated medical guidance due to poor connectivity. 
This use case proposes a maternal care support system with SevisPass Digital ID authentication. Verified health workers log in and access trusted maternal care resources. The system allows workers to download step-by-step instructional videos on safe delivery, danger signs, and complication management for offline use. Health workers record patient outcomes and share updates with provincial hospitals when connectivity is available. The platform also provides practical guidance on how to use available resources in low-supply settings. Authorities can track key health indicators and monitor rural centers. This approach is uniquely differentiated from generic digital health applications, such as WHO's Safe Delivery App, because it operates entirely offline and in a resource-constrained environment.

Title: Secure digital diploma issuance and verification system using Sevis Pass DPI
KeyTerms: Digital Credentials, Certificate Issuance, Public Key Infrastructure, Verifiable Credentials, Data Exchange, Credential Verification, Digital Inclusion
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 1 - Students can present this digital diploma to an employer via SevisPass Wallet using SevisPass digital
Description:
Forged educational certificates create a national crisis in Papua New Guinea. They reduce employer trust, weaken the value of education, and enable fraud. The Department of Education and tertiary institutions do not have a secure digital system to issue tamper-proof diplomas. 
The proposed use case is a Business-to-Government (B2G) software system to solve this problem. This system will enable educational institutions to issue digital credentials directly to citizens' official SevisPass wallets. The platform serves as a secure backend and enables institutions to act as trusted issuers within the SevisPass ecosystem. 
The workflow operates as follows: A registrar at a tertiary institution, or in the Department of Education, logs in with secure credentials and manages student records. After graduation, the registrar selects “Issue digital diploma.” The system creates a digitally signed credential and sends it through SevisDEx to the student’s SevisPass wallet. Employers can verify the diploma authenticity instantly using their own credentials through this system.

Title: Secure education certificate verification system using SevisPass
KeyTerms: Student Identity, Certificate Verification, Credential Security, Digital Certification, QR Code Authentication, Fraud Prevention, Public Key Infrastructure (PKI)
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 1 - PKI-based authentication using SevisPass ID for school administrators and organizations accessing student records
Description:
Papua New Guinea relies on paper-based student registration and manual data entry. This creates errors such as misspelled names and inconsistent records. Schools also struggle to confirm student identity. Anyone can edit and reproduce Grade 8, 10, and 12 certificates because they lack digital security. Verification relies on manual checks, which take time and erode trust. 
This use case idea proposes a comprehensive national student identity system that will use SevisPass Digital ID, linked to the SevisDEx data exchange platform. Schools register students through a parent-assisted online registration portal or dedicated school-based registration stations, ensuring verified identity before students take school certificate exams. The National Department of Education will issue passing certificates with a QR code linked to a digitally signed record using its public key infrastructure (PKI). Any registered organization can scan the QR code and verify student details using PKI certificates. Through this verification process, the respective organization confirms the authenticity of the student's name, school, year, and result status.

Title: Digital platform for emergency response and public safety
KeyTerms: Public Safety, Crime Prevention, Community Security, Real-Time Alerts, GPS Location, Data Sharing, Digital Inclusion, Privacy & Data Protection
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 0 - No verification needed for accessing emergency services and location data sharing, just pressing the panic button will activate the emergency response services
Description:
Certain regions of Papua New Guinea face public safety issues. Violence, theft, and harassment often go unreported. Slow emergency response and the lack of a reliable online incident reporting platform worsen the issue. Women and youth face a higher risk. 
The proposed mobile application will provide an emergency support and reporting facility. It integrates SevisPass digital ID to verify individuals and create their profiles with emergency details. The system confirms identity during registration and login through biometric checks. This step prevents fake accounts and reduces false alerts.
When the emergency button on the app is activated, the app overrides the device's usual phone settings to capture live audio and location. It sends this data, along with details, to emergency responders. Emergency responders will receive alerts from verified users and hence shall act with greater confidence.
This enables emergency responders and local authorities to act with greater confidence, which leads to faster response times and improved public trust. The app captures live audio and device location. It sends this data with verified identity details to responders. The system shares only the required information and protects sensitive data. Users maintain one trusted identity across services such as reporting, safety updates, and emergency support. This improves response time and builds public trust.

Title: Digital PMV system for driver verification and real-time bus tracking.
KeyTerms: Driver Verification, Fleet Management, Safety, Real-Time Tracking, Mobile Device, Community Engagement
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multi-factor authentication for vehicle owner and driver  for safety and tracking
Description:
Papua New Guinea faces safety and accountability issues in the public motor vehicle (PMV) sector. Many buses run without proper permits or in unsafe conditions. This puts passengers and fellow travelers at risk. Owners also face theft, misuse, and poor service due to limited monitoring. 
The digital PMV system use case idea will use Sevis Pass digital ID to verify driver identities and link them to the public vehicle they will operate. Each vehicle will have an ownership and maintenance record in a digital profile, linked to the owner's Sevis Pass ID. The driver will confirm their identity through multi-factor authentication before they operate a vehicle.
The system tracks buses in real time through onboard GPS. Owners can monitor location and driving behavior. 
Through onboard GPS, the system tracks buses in real time, which allows owners to monitor location and driving behavior.
It allows passengers to verify the driver by scanning the QR code on the vehicle's exterior, which links to a real-time status page for the authenticated driver. They can also send SMS alerts to report safety concerns. This system improves transparency and safety across the sector.

Title: Improving child TB treatment adherence under an incentive program
KeyTerms: TB Management, Child Health, Treatment Adherence, Social Protection, Digital Inclusion, Incentive Management, Data Sharing, Contact Tracing
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 1 - OTP based authentication for treatment adherence verification and sharing this data with healthcare worker and for incentive approval systems
Description:
The World Health Organization classifies Papua New Guinea as a high tuberculosis burden country. 
Children account for about 26% of reported cases. Many of these patients stop treatment due to stigma and social barriers. As a result, “lost-to-follow-up” cases continue to spread TB.
This use case utilizes SevisPass and Sevis Wallet to solve this problem. When a child visits a clinic, staff verify the former's identity through SevisPass and link the child's medical records to their SevisPass profile using the Medical Record Number (MR#). From this point onward, every test, treatment decision, and visit to a healthcare center can be tracked via the child's SevisPass.
Health workers track adherence through regular check-ins. If a child misses any check-ins, workers respond quickly and trace them. On completing the mandatory visits, the system transfers incentives to caregivers through linked bank accounts. This approach can improve outcomes for nearly 400,000 children in Papua New Guinea.

Title: A unified digital health record system for PNG
KeyTerms: Healthcare, Digital Health Records, Patient Identity, USSD, SMS, Offline-Ready, Data Sharing, Interoperability, Rural Access
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multi-factor authentication for clinician and administrator access to health records (ID and OTP)
Description:
Papua New Guinea relies on paper-based patient records that often get lost, damaged, or remain unavailable during emergencies. This creates serious challenges for rural communities that travel long distances to access care.
This use case idea proposes to establish a unified national health information platform. It uses SevisPass Digital ID to verify patient identity and create their secure health profiles. Each registered individual will have a digital health profile accessible across participating hospitals and clinics. Healthcare providers confirm identity via biometric authentication on the platform rather than manual forms. Rural clinics can operate offline and sync data after connectivity returns.
To ensure inclusive access, the system will also include SMS reminders and a simple USSD service for individuals without smartphones or internet access. Patients receive notifications via SMS to visit the clinic or log in to a portal to view secure results. The system utilizes SMS for push notifications (reminders) and an interactive USSD menu, allowing users to query their status or book appointments.

Title: Biometric-based digital farmer identity for inclusive and resilient agriculture in West Africa
KeyTerms: Biometric Authentication, Blockchain, Land Rights, Financial Inclusion, Fraud Prevention, Supply Chain Traceability, Accessibility
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multi-factor biometric authentication for subsidy access and financial transactions
Description:
West African farmers (70% of the population) face widespread fraud and exclusion in availing financial support from the government. About 40% of agricultural subsidies are diverted by intermediaries and land conflicts account for 60% of rural disputes. Nearly 80% of farmers remain outside the formal financial system.
This use case idea proposes a platform that leverages a biometric authentication service provided by the national digital identity system to address these challenges. Farmers must register on the platform using their national digital ID. Once registered, a farmer can authenticate with fingerprints at mobile kiosks or use USSD with a PIN or OTP to link their bank account with the platform. This allows direct subsidy payments and removes intermediaries.
The farmer’s digital ID is also used to authenticate them and link their profile to their land title records. This platform also enables the sharing of farmers’ credit scores with financial organizations, based on past data.
The harvest traceability of individual farmers can be done using this platform, which enables access to microloans for unbanked farmers. Each harvest receives a digital "product passport" linked to the farmer ID, enabling market access via QR codes. Farmers can use USSD on basic phones without internet, as long as there is GSM coverage. For areas with no network connectivity, agent devices support offline data capture and synchronization when connectivity becomes available.

Title: Digital ID and AI platform for smallholder farmer empowerment and Financial Access
KeyTerms: Digital Innovation, AI-Driven Agriculture, Financial Inclusion, Local Language Support, Climate Resilience, Sustainable Farming
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multi-factor authentication for subsidy applications and financial transaction access
Description:
Small farmers in Africa face challenges due to the absence of official documents. These barriers prevent them from proving their financial status. As a result, they struggle to access formal credit and subsidy programs. These barriers isolate them from markets and limit their access to extension support.
This use case proposes a digital ID-based platform that uses AI to support farmers. Each farmer receives a unique farmer ID linked to their national digital ID. This farmer ID stores land and produce-related data, such as land size, crop type, and production history. The platform provides personalized advice on irrigation, soil health, planting cycles, and pest control. It supports local languages to improve access in rural areas.
It also simplifies access to subsidies and microcredit through AI-based eligibility checks for various institutional lenders.
The platform will also include training modules, disease-detection tools, weather alerts, and other features to improve farmers’ productivity.

Title: MOSIP-powered driver licensing system
KeyTerms: Public Services, Corruption Prevention, Digital Identity, Fraud Reduction, Process Efficiency, Citizen Access
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 1 - QR code-based single-factor authentication for license management and traffic verification systems
Description:
Many African countries still use manual, paper-based driver licensing systems that are vulnerable to fraud and data errors. Traffic officers cannot detect suspended driving licenses or fake permits because they lack reliable roadside verification tools.

Transport authorities can use national digital ID authentication services to provide roadside verification. This use case proposes that they launch a digital licensing system. When a citizen applies for a driving license, the system uses the national digital ID e-KYC service to verify their identity. All the driving licenses will be linked to the respective citizens’ national digital ID. In the digital driving license database, the digital ID number will be linked to additional data, such as the license expiry date and license category. 

The authority then issues a digital license as a cryptographically signed credential. Citizens can store it in a digital license app or carry it as a QR code card. Traffic police can scan the code with a dedicated app to verify these licenses instantly, even without an internet connection.
During application or renewal, the authority confirms identity through biometric authentication to retrieve past license data issued against that digital ID. This step reduces impersonation and duplicate records.

Title: Smart battery monitor and power optimization system for mobile devices
KeyTerms: Mobile Technology, Energy Efficiency, Technology Accessibility, Digital Inclusion, Power Management
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 1 - Optional account authentication for premium features and cloud synchronization
Description:
Africa has seen a sharp rise in smartphone use, and this trend will continue. However, users face frequent battery drain. Most devices lose charge within 7–8 hours as they age. Many apps run in the background and strain hardware and storage.

This use case proposes an application that acts as an authenticator. It integrates digital ID across all apps on the device. It streamlines user experience and improves device performance. The app enables digital ID-based registration for third-party apps and controls cookies and settings based on user preferences. It offers a single platform for credential verification and authentication.

The app also monitors battery use, tracks battery health, and sends alerts. It identifies high-drain apps and suggests fixes. It analyzes usage patterns and activates power-saving modes when needed. It delivers full battery management without extra hardware. The application has an accessible, user-friendly interface that facilitates the use of digital ID applications on mobile devices without interruption. It combines existing battery optimization features, such as Greenify, AccuBattery, and DU Battery Saver into a single comprehensive solution.

Title: Biometric authentication and eKYC for SIM card and mobile money registrations
KeyTerms: Mobile Money, Fraud Prevention, Biometric Authentication, SIM Registration, Digital Inclusion, Cybersecurity
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multi-factor authentication for rechecking of account verification
Description:
Many countries in Eastern Africa, such as Malawi, face a growing problem of mobile money fraud. The current agent-based registration process for SIM cards and fintech accounts onboard users without strong identity verification. This gap allows fraudsters to create fake accounts.

This use case idea proposes to link SIM cards and mobile money accounts directly to a person’s national digital ID using MOSIP’s identity authentication and e-KYC services. At SIM issuance, the telecom agent will authenticate the user by using biometrics, such as a fingerprint or facial image, along with the national digital ID credential. The system sends this data to the national ID platform, which performs a one-to-one biometric match to confirm identity.

The platform will also require fresh biometric checks for high-risk actions, such as SIM swaps or mobile money account registrations linked to the phone numbers. The user must also repeat biometric authentication within 24 hours to confirm these actions and avoid the creation of fake accounts. Any mismatch or repeated attempts trigger fraud alerts and block suspicious accounts.

Title: Digital ID-enabled warehouse system to reduce post-harvest loss in agriculture
KeyTerms: Storage Technology, Renewable Energy, Rural Development, Financial Inclusion, Digital Marketplace
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Face-based authentication in case of the government audit of the warehouse or for product verification in case of the digital market access
Description:
In recent years, post-harvest losses in Senegal's agriculture sector have been estimated at 50–70%. These losses force farmers to sell produce at very low prices, often to predatory lenders, while consumers struggle to access quality food at affordable costs.

This use case proposes an application that will use a national digital ID-linked storage system to enable farmers to access refrigerated and dry warehouses. Farmers use their national digital ID to create verified profiles on the mobile application. The app displays available storage space in nearby government-owned or government-registered warehouses. Based on estimated harvest volumes, farmers can reserve storage space in advance by paying a booking fee. Confirmed bookings include a time window to reach the warehouse to prevent unused capacity.

Participating warehouses offer access to an online marketplace for a service fee, enabling farmers to sell produce when prices are favorable. To ensure transparency, all stored food grains must be digitally linked to the farmer’s ID. Warehouses that fail to comply face penalties or deregistration.

Title: A digital ID-based agricultural marketplace for West African farmers
KeyTerms: Agriculture, Digital Marketplace, Fraud Prevention, Financial Inclusion, Digital Inclusion, AI Chatbot, Training & Development, Rural Access
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 1 - OTP based authentication for marketplace access and agricultural transactions
Description:
Farmers in West Africa have limited access to markets, therefore lack real-time information on competitive prices for their crops. This use case proposes a digital agriculture marketplace platform. It features verified farmer profiles linked to their national digital IDs.

Farmers register on the platform with an OTP-based verification service. They use the national digital ID to create their secure farmer profile. Verified farmers can list their produce for sale. They include details such as crop type, quantity, quality, location, and photos. Buyers from different regions also register as verified users on this platform, which builds trust on both sides of the transaction. On-ground agents authenticate uploaded information in response to requests from verified buyers.

The platform will also feature a resource hub that shares agricultural news, best farming practices, weather updates, and information on grants and support programs from trusted institutions. A mobile-first design and local language support ensure easy access for rural users.

Title: Integrating national digital ID for donor verification and blood inventory tracking
KeyTerms: Social Protection, Digital Identity, Mobile Device, Voice Interface, Accessibility, Emergency Response, Predictive Analytics
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 1 - Single-factor PIN-based authentication for donor access and donation history
Description:
Blood banks across West and Central African countries often lack reliable digital records to identify repeat donors, track blood units, and quickly find compatible blood during emergencies.

This use case proposes the establishment of a national digital ID–enabled donor registry. Each donor will be registered by the blood bank using facial biometric authentication services provided by the national digital ID. Each verified individual national digital ID will be linked to the unique donor ID, which serves as a portable digital health credential. The proposed donor registry stores these IDs, along with essential data such as blood group, donation history, and the availability status.

Every blood unit has key details tagged as a QR code, which include the donor's ID, collection date, blood type, and expiration date. This ensures full traceability from donor to blood unit by all the stakeholders.

When hospitals raise emergency requests, blood banks use the registry to send digital alerts to eligible donors through a mobile or web application. The donor confirms his/her availability using digital ID authentication, enabling immediate matching.

Title: Digital ID-based land registration and solar energy allotment to farmers
KeyTerms: Agriculture, Energy Access, Land Rights, Blockchain, IoT Monitoring, Renewable Energy, Climate Resilience, Financial Inclusion
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multi-factor CNIE authentication for land registration and solar kit access (Facial Recognition and OTP)
Description:
Rural areas in Morocco lack reliable access to electricity. The government addressed this gap through renewable energy programs that install solar energy systems on farmland. However, these programs face challenges due to the absence of formal land titles in rural areas.

This use case proposes an application that links Morocco’s national digital ID (CNIE) to the land registration process in rural areas. This will help the government allocate solar installations fairly based on land ownership. Government agents map land boundaries using this application during field visits. The identity of the landholder is verified using CNIE-based facial recognition. Once verified, each parcel of land is assigned a unique digital land ID linked to the CNIE of the legal landholder.

Land parcels are also categorized as farmland, residential areas, or community land based on their use, which is recorded against the respective digital land ID. The energy department analyzes land ownership and installs solar systems on eligible properties based on size, use, and productivity. During installation, officials verify land ownership using CNIE-based verification services.

Title: Driving integration and inclusion in agriculture services through digital identity
KeyTerms: Digital Inclusion & Accessibility, Biometric Verification, Blockchain, Financial Services, Supply Chain Transparency, Training & Development
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 1 - OTP based authentication for financial services access and subsidy distribution
Description:
In many East African countries, farmers lack a single digital platform for farming advice, loans, insurance, or markets. Consequently, they lack accurate information on farm inputs. They also struggle to get credit and find it hard to sell their produce. This use case proposes a digital farmer registry linked to the national digital ID. 

Trained agents enroll farmers using national digital ID credentials and biometric authentication during in-person visits. The registry links each national digital ID to its respective farmer profile. This profile consists of verified demographic data, land ownership records, crop types, farm size, and farming practices. It also links financial information, such as bank account details, income history, and credit indicators. These details create a complete agroeconomic profile. 

Farmers can securely share their profiles with approved public and private entities through OTP-based consent. For instance, banking institutions use this profile to assess farmer creditworthiness, and governments use it to distribute subsidies directly. The registry can be further developed to ease supply chain issues faced by farmers.

Title: Digital ID-based personal health records system to share healthcare data among healthcare organizations
KeyTerms: Digital Health Records, Patient Identity, Emergency Care, Biometric authentication, Medical Data Integration, Public Key Infrastructure (PKI)
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multifactor authentication by patients at the healthcare centers
Description:
Healthcare providers lack direct access to patients’ reliable medical records. This issue causes delay in emergency care, misdiagnosis, and treatment errors. This use case proposes to link comprehensive personal health records (PHRs) with an individual's digital IDs for streamlined healthcare accessibility. 

By registering on the proposed portal, citizens can link their digital IDs securely to PHRs. These records contain identification details, medical history, medications, allergies, blood type, immunization history, diagnostic data, and legal health documents, among others. A national system stores this health data securely with proper cybersecurity protocols. Authorized healthcare institutions access it through a PKI framework. This framework uses a dedicated national and/or regional healthcare certificate authorities (CAs). 

All data access requires patient consent via biometric authentication at the healthcare institution. This ensures secure access to sensitive records. These digital health records travel through an encrypted channel. Healthcare providers access only data subsets based on their role and the patient consent. These transactions are subject to audit, which ensures accountability.

Title: Labor and unorganized workers registration (E-Shram Portal)
KeyTerms: Informal sector, Worker welfare, Social protection, National database
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Aadhaar-based biometric verification
Description: Digital ID is used to create a comprehensive National Database of Unorganised Workers (NDUW), verified and seeded with Aadhaar, enabling delivery of targeted welfare schemes to India's vast informal workforce. The E-Shram portal, launched on August 26, 2021, by the Ministry of Labour & Employment in collaboration with UIDAI, registers unorganized workers including construction workers, migrant workers, gig and platform workers, street vendors, domestic workers, agricultural laborers, and others who lack formal employment contracts and social security coverage. During registration, workers provide their Aadhaar number for identity verification and demographic data auto-population from the UIDAI database, along with occupation details, skill sets, and bank account information. The system generates a unique 12-digit Universal Account Number (UAN) for each registered worker and issues a digital E-Shram card accessible via mobile app. As of December 2024, the E-Shram portal has registered 30,48,02,313 (304.8 million) unorganized workers, creating the world's largest database of informal sector workers. The Aadhaar-verified database enables the government to identify beneficiaries and deliver welfare schemes directly to registered workers' bank accounts using the JAM (Jan Dhan-Aadhaar-Mobile) trinity. As of the latest data, 12 central government schemes have been integrated with E-Shram including Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY) providing accidental insurance coverage, Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY) providing life insurance, Atal Pension Yojana (APY) for old-age pension, National Pension Scheme (NPS), health insurance schemes, skill development programs, and maternity benefits.

Title: Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises registration (Udyam Portal)
KeyTerms: MSME registration, Business licensing, Entrepreneurship, eKYC
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Aadhaar-based e-KYC authentication
Description: Digital ID is used to verify the identity of business owners or authorized signatories during MSME registration, enabling a paperless, online, and cost-free registration process. The Udyam Registration Portal, launched on July 1, 2020, by the Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises in collaboration with UIDAI, provides a unified, simplified registration system for MSMEs across India. The portal uses Aadhaar-based e-KYC to instantly verify the identity of entrepreneurs applying for MSME registration, eliminating the need for physical document submission and enabling self-registration from anywhere. Business owners provide their Aadhaar number during registration, which validates their identity against the UIDAI database and auto-fills demographic details including name and address. The system also integrates with the Income Tax Department and GSTN (Goods and Services Tax Network) to auto-populate business financial data including investment in plant and machinery and annual turnover based on PAN and GSTN, further simplifying the registration process. Upon successful registration, enterprises receive a permanent 16-digit Udyam Registration Number (URN) and a digital certificate. As of May 27, 2025, a total of 6,40,94,133 (64.09 million) MSMEs have registered on the Udyam portal. Udyam registration enables MSMEs to access various government schemes and benefits including the Credit Guarantee Scheme (CGTMSE) for collateral-free loans, Public Procurement Policy mandating 25% government procurement from MSMEs, subsidies and incentives for technology upgrades, priority sector lending status from banks, and protection under the Delayed Payment Act.

Title: Employee Provident Fund (EPFO)
KeyTerms: Social security, Retirement savings, Provident fund, Claims processing
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Aadhaar-based authentication
Description: Digital ID is linked to Universal Account Numbers (UANs) to facilitate seamless withdrawal and claims processing for employee provident fund benefits. The Employees' Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO), under the Ministry of Labour & Employment, in collaboration with UIDAI, has integrated Aadhaar with the UAN system to streamline provident fund operations for formal sector employees. UAN is a unique 12-digit number allotted to every EPFO member, which remains the same throughout their career even when changing employers, serving as a portable and permanent provident fund account identifier. Aadhaar-UAN linking enables employees to verify their identity during provident fund operations including EPF withdrawals, pension claims, transfer of funds between employers, and updating KYC details. Members can link their Aadhaar to UAN through the EPFO Unified Member Portal or UMANG app by providing their Aadhaar number and validating it through OTP authentication sent to their Aadhaar-registered mobile number. As of September 2021, 94% of UANs have been seeded with Aadhaar, representing millions of formal sector workers. The Aadhaar-UAN integration has enabled several innovations including paperless and presence-less claim settlement where employees can submit EPF withdrawal claims online without visiting EPFO offices or submitting physical documents, with claims processed and approved within days based on Aadhaar authentication. The system also supports the EPFO's Automation of Field Offices (AFO) initiative and enables auto-settlement of claims under specified limits. The Aadhaar-EPFO integration has transformed social security administration in India by enabling instant, paperless provident fund claim settlement without employer attestation for Aadhaar-verified members, reducing claim processing time from months to days or even hours, eliminating the need for physical document submission and office visits, preventing fraudulent claims through biometric identity verification, facilitating portability of provident fund accounts across employers, supporting financial security for over 70 million formal sector workers, and ensuring retirement savings reach rightful beneficiaries promptly.

Title: Railway ticket booking (IRCTC)
KeyTerms: Rail travel, e-Ticketing, Tatkal booking, Passenger verification
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Aadhaar OTP-based authentication
Description: Digital ID is used for passenger identity verification during railway ticket booking, particularly for Tatkal (last-minute) reservations, and for adding passengers to traveler master lists. Introduced in 2016, the Indian Railway Catering and Tourism Corporation (IRCTC) in collaboration with UIDAI implemented Aadhaar-based authentication for railway ticket bookings to prevent ticket touting, reduce fraudulent bookings, and ensure genuine passengers receive reservations. For Tatkal ticket bookings, a quota allowing last-minute reservations starting one day before journey date and highly susceptible to automated bulk booking by touts, Aadhaar OTP-based authentication became mandatory. Passengers booking Tatkal tickets must provide their Aadhaar number and authenticate using a One-Time Password (OTP) sent to their Aadhaar-registered mobile number, ensuring only verified individuals can make such bookings. Additionally, passengers can add frequently traveled companions to their IRCTC master list by providing Aadhaar numbers, enabling faster future bookings with pre-verified passenger details. During ticket checking on trains, Ticket Examiners (TEs) verify passenger identity by matching travel documents with government-issued IDs including Aadhaar. The Aadhaar integration addresses the chronic problem of ticket black marketing where touts used automated software to bulk-book Tatkal tickets and resell them at inflated prices, depriving genuine passengers. Following implementation, Indian Railways reported significant reduction in touting activities.

Title: Highway Toll Payment - FASTag (KYC)
KeyTerms: Electronic toll collection, RFID, eKYC, Digital payments, Transport infrastructure
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Aadhaar-based e-KYC authentication
Description: Digital ID is used for electronic Know Your Customer (e-KYC) verification during FASTag registration, enabling instant issuance of electronic toll collection tags. FASTag, launched in 2016 by the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) in collaboration with the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI), UIDAI, and authorized banks, is a Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) device affixed to vehicle windshields that enables automatic deduction of toll charges without requiring vehicles to stop at toll plazas. To obtain a FASTag, vehicle owners must complete KYC verification, for which Aadhaar-based e-KYC has become the preferred method due to its speed and convenience. Customers can register for FASTag through multiple channels including bank branches, point-of-sale locations at toll plazas, online banking portals, and mobile apps by providing their Aadhaar number for instant identity verification against the UIDAI database. The Aadhaar e-KYC process retrieves the applicant's verified name, address, and photograph, eliminating the need for physical document submission and enabling instant FASTag activation. The system also requires vehicle registration details to link the FASTag to the specific vehicle. FASTag became mandatory for all class M & N vehicles in India from February 2021, driving rapid adoption. As of April 2025, over 10.85 crore (108.5 million) FASTags have been issued, with Aadhaar playing a key role in enabling seamless e-KYC during registration.

Title: CoWIN (COVID-19 Vaccination)
KeyTerms: Vaccination management, Immunization, Certificate issuance, Public health
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Aadhaar-based verification or alternative ID verification
Description: Digital ID is used as a primary identification method for booking vaccination appointments and issuing digital vaccination certificates. The COVID-19 Vaccine Intelligence Network (CoWIN), launched in 2021 by the Ministry of Health & Family Welfare in collaboration with UIDAI, is India's digital platform for managing the world's largest COVID-19 vaccination program. CoWIN enables citizens to register for vaccination, book appointments at nearby vaccination centers, and receive digital vaccination certificates. Beneficiaries can register using multiple forms of identification including Aadhaar, driving license, PAN card, passport, voter ID, pension passbook, NPR smart card, or other government-issued photo IDs, with Aadhaar serving as the preferred and most widely used ID due to its universal coverage and instant verification capabilities. After registration using Aadhaar or alternative ID, beneficiaries receive SMS notifications for appointment confirmation and can walk into vaccination centers where their identity is verified against the registered details. Upon vaccination, the system instantly generates a digitally verifiable vaccination certificate containing the beneficiary's name, ID details, vaccine type, dose number, vaccination date, and a QR code for international verification. As of the program's conclusion, over 2.2 billion vaccination doses were administered through the CoWIN platform, making it one of the largest and fastest vaccination drives in human history. The CoWIN-Aadhaar integration enabled India to achieve massive vaccination scale by providing real-time tracking of vaccine inventory and distribution across 1 million+ vaccination sessions, preventing duplicate vaccinations through unique ID verification, enabling beneficiaries to receive dose reminders via SMS to Aadhaar-linked mobile numbers, generating internationally recognized digital vaccination certificates crucial for travel, facilitating transparent monitoring of vaccination coverage across demographics and geographies, and supporting India's successful COVID-19 response that achieved over 90% adult population coverage with at least one dose.

Title: Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA)
KeyTerms: Digital health, Health records, Unified Health Interface
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Aadhaar-based authentication (biometric or OTP)
Description: Digital ID is voluntarily used to create a unique health identifier that links and stores an individual's health records across healthcare providers. The Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA), formerly known as Health ID, was launched in 2021 by the National Health Authority (NHA) under the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) in collaboration with UIDAI. ABHA is a 14-digit unique health identification number that serves as a digital gateway to an individual's longitudinal health records, enabling seamless sharing of health information across hospitals, diagnostic centers, pharmacies, insurance companies, and telemedicine platforms. Citizens can voluntarily create their ABHA using Aadhaar-based authentication (via OTP or biometric) or through mobile number-based verification without Aadhaar, ensuring privacy and user choice following the Supreme Court's guidelines on voluntary Aadhaar use. The ABHA system links to personal health records (PHRs) stored in digital lockers accessible through the ABHA mobile app or participating healthcare applications, allowing patients to consent-based sharing of medical history, prescriptions, diagnostic reports, discharge summaries, and immunization records with healthcare providers. As of December 10, 2024, a total of 71.16 crore (711.6 million) Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts have been created, demonstrating widespread adoption of India's digital health infrastructure.

Title: Tax ID – Permanent Account Number (PAN) and Aadhaar linking
KeyTerms: Tax identification, Financial transactions, Tax compliance, Identity deduplication
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Aadhaar-based verification and deduplication
Description: Digital ID is used to verify and uniquely link Permanent Account Number (PAN) cards to Aadhaar, creating a unified tax identification system. Operational since 2017 and made mandatory in 2019 (with extensions), the Income Tax Department in collaboration with UIDAI requires all PAN holders to link their PAN with their Aadhaar number. PAN is a 10-character alphanumeric identifier issued by the Income Tax Department to track financial transactions and tax compliance, while Aadhaar provides biometric-based unique identity. The PAN-Aadhaar linkage serves to eliminate duplicate and fake PAN cards, ensure unique tax identification, and enable cross-verification of taxpayer information. Taxpayers can link their PAN-Aadhaar through multiple channels: the Income Tax e-filing portal, SMS (by sending a message with PAN and Aadhaar numbers to a designated number), or through Aadhaar-PAN linking facilities at banks and service centers. As of January 2024, more than 510 million PANs have been successfully linked with Aadhaar. The Income Tax Act was amended to make PAN-Aadhaar linking mandatory, with unlinked PANs becoming inoperative, meaning they cannot be used for financial transactions such as opening bank accounts, purchasing property, or filing income tax returns. The PAN-Aadhaar linkage system has strengthened India's tax administration by eliminating duplicate and bogus PAN cards estimated in millions, enabling detection of tax evasion through identification of individuals holding multiple PANs, supporting the government's efforts to expand the tax base and improve compliance, facilitating seamless verification of taxpayer identity across financial and tax systems, reducing identity fraud in high-value financial transactions requiring PAN, and creating a robust, unique taxpayer identification infrastructure that integrates India's tax and identity ecosystems.

Title: Voluntary use of Aadhaar for passport application
KeyTerms: Passport issuance, Travel document, Identity verification, Consular services
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Aadhaar-based identity validation
Description: Digital ID is voluntarily used to validate applicant identity during passport application processes before the Passport Verification/Granting Officer. Operational since 2015, the Ministry of External Affairs in collaboration with UIDAI allows Indian citizens to provide their Aadhaar number as an identity and address proof document during passport application through the Passport Seva portal. Applicants can voluntarily mention their Aadhaar number in the online passport application form, which can then be used by Passport Seva Kendras (PSKs) and Passport Offices to verify the applicant's identity and address details against the UIDAI database during document verification. Following the Supreme Court's 2018 judgment on Aadhaar, the use of Aadhaar for passport applications is voluntary, and applicants can submit alternative identity and address proof documents if they choose not to use Aadhaar. However, providing Aadhaar can simplify the application process by reducing the number of supporting documents required and expediting verification. Applicants who provide Aadhaar may not need to submit additional proof of identity (such as voter ID, driving license, or PAN card) and proof of address (such as utility bills or rental agreements), as Aadhaar serves both purposes. The Aadhaar integration supports streamlined passport application processing by reducing documentation requirements for applicants who opt to use Aadhaar, enabling faster identity and address verification by Passport Officers, reducing manual scrutiny of multiple physical documents, preventing duplicate passport applications through Aadhaar uniqueness, and supporting India's goal of providing accessible passport services while maintaining security and preventing fraudulent applications.

Title: Property registration and land records
KeyTerms: Land administration, Property rights, eKYC, Stamp duty, Real estate
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Aadhaar-based biometric and e-KYC authentication
Description: Digital ID is used for identity verification of buyers and sellers during property registration and land transactions. Operational since 2015, various State Governments across India have integrated Aadhaar-based e-KYC into their property registration systems in collaboration with UIDAI to verify the identities of parties involved in property transactions. During property registration at Sub-Registrar offices, buyers, sellers, and witnesses must authenticate their identities using Aadhaar-based biometric verification (fingerprint or iris scan) or e-KYC, which retrieves verified demographic details directly from the UIDAI database. This system has been implemented in multiple states including Karnataka, Maharashtra, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, and others as part of their Digital India Land Records Modernization Programme (DILRMP). Aadhaar authentication ensures that only the genuine property owner can execute sale deeds or transfer documents, preventing impersonation and fraudulent transactions. The integration also facilitates linkage with the Digital India Land Records Modernization Programme (DILRMP) and digitization of land records under initiatives like SVAMITVA (Survey of Villages and Mapping with Improvised Technology in Village Areas).

Title: Filing income-tax returns (ITR e-verification)
KeyTerms: Tax compliance, e-Filing, Digital verification, Revenue administration
